description: Address code review findings: fix data race on findings vector by adding per-scan mutex, wire YARA engine into processFile, strip file permissions after quarantine move, and make Quick scan paths configurable instead of hard-coded.
steps: Add a std::mutex findingsMutex to the ActiveScan struct in ScanEngine.h to guard the findings vector separately from scansMutex_
Add a defaultQuickScanPaths_ field and a setter method to ScanEngine so Quick scan paths are configurable
In ScanEngine.cpp processFile, use scan.findingsMutex instead of scansMutex_ when pushing to findings
In ScanEngine.cpp processFile, add a call to yaraEngine_.matchFile() and handle YARA matches
In ScanEngine.cpp getScanStatus/getScanResults, use the per-scan findingsMutex when reading findings
In ScanEngine.cpp getTargetsForType, use the configurable paths for Quick scan instead of hard-coded values
In QuarantineManager.cpp quarantine(), call std::filesystem::permissions() after the file move to restrict access (remove all permissions except owner read)
Build and test to verify all fixes work correctly
acceptance_criteria: ActiveScan has its own mutex for findings, separate from the map-level scansMutex_
processFile uses the per-scan mutex for findings
processFile calls yaraEngine_.matchFile() and records any YARA matches in findings
QuarantineManager strips permissions on quarantined files after move
Quick scan paths are configurable via a setter or constructor parameter, not hard-coded
findings: All four priority review issues addressed: (1) Per-scan findingsMutex added to ActiveScan struct, used in processFile and getScanStatus/getScanResults instead of scansMutex_. (2) YARA engine now invoked via yaraEngine_.matchFile() in processFile when engine status is Ready and rules are loaded. (3) Quarantine permissions stripped to owner_read after move using std::filesystem::permissions with perm_options::replace. (4) Quick/Full scan paths made configurable via setQuickScanPaths/setFullScanPaths setters with platform-aware defaults as fallback. Build passes cleanly, all tests pass.
